BASE.name: ATTACK_VECTOR
BASE.justification: The attacker can exploit the open redirect remotely by crafting malicious URLs delivered over the network.

BASE.name: ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
BASE.justification: Exploitation only requires supplying an untrusted redirect destination without special conditions.

BASE.name: PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
BASE.justification: No authentication or special privileges are required to exploit the redirect.

BASE.name: USER_INTERACTION
BASE.justification: A victim must click or follow the crafted redirect link.

BASE.name: SCOPE
BASE.justification: The redirect occurs within the same application boundary without executing attacker code.

BASE.name: CONFIDENTIALITY
BASE.justification: The redirect itself does not expose data but can facilitate phishing or credential harvesting.

BASE.name: INTEGRITY
BASE.justification: User navigation and trust can be manipulated, but no direct data modification occurs.

BASE.name: AVAILABILITY
BASE.justification: The vulnerability does not affect service availability.

TEMPORAL.name: EXPLOIT_CODE_MATURITY
TEMPORAL.justification: Open redirect exploits are trivial to demonstrate and commonly documented.

TEMPORAL.name: REMEDIATION_LEVEL
TEMPORAL.justification: Redirect risks can be mitigated through input validation or allowlisting, but may require broader refactoring.

TEMPORAL.name: REPORT_CONFIDENCE
TEMPORAL.justification: The issue is well understood and reliably detected by static analysis.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_VECTOR
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: In the deployed environment, the redirect remains remotely exploitable via network-based links.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: No environmental factors increase the difficulty of exploiting the redirect.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Attackers do not require authentication in the target environment.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_USER_INTERACTION
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Successful exploitation still depends on a user following the malicious link.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_SCOPE
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The redirect does not cause execution outside the application’s trust boundary.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_CONFIDENTIALITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Indirect confidentiality impact may occur through phishing, but no direct data exposure is inherent.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_INTEGRITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: User trust and navigation flow can be manipulated, but backend data integrity remains intact.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_AVAILABILITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: The redirect does not degrade or interrupt application availability.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: CONFIDENTIALITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Protection against phishing and credential theft is important in most environments.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: INTEGRITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Maintaining trusted navigation and preventing user deception is a moderate priority.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: AVAILABILITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Availability is not a concern for this class of vulnerability.
